message:
feat(messaging): implement cut-certified mobility primitives and fix Sublist cache correctness

Major expansion of the messaging subsystem:

- cut.rs: Replace 8-line placeholder with 843-line implementation of
  cut-certified mobility primitives for FABRIC subject cells. Introduces
  ConsumerStateDigest, CapsuleDigest for deterministic state fingerprinting,
  CutCertificate capturing minimum state for cell authority transfer,
  MobilityOperation enum for lawful cell moves, CertifiedMobility trait
  for type-safe application of mobility operations, and CutMobilityError
  for structured failure reporting. This enables hot cell migration without
  hand-wavy drain heuristics — the caller mints a certificate from the
  current cell then applies a lawful operation yielding the next SubjectCell.

- mod.rs: Wire cut module into public API gated behind messaging-fabric
  feature; re-export CapsuleDigest, CertifiedMobility, ConsumerStateDigest,
  CutCertificate, CutMobilityError, and MobilityOperation.

- subject.rs: Fix Sublist cache correctness — CacheEntry now stores raw
  matched subscriber info (plain_ids and queue_groups) separately instead
  of a pre-selected SublistResult. Queue group round-robin selection runs
  fresh on each lookup call rather than being cached, which was causing
  the same subscriber to be selected repeatedly when cache hits occurred.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
